scenarios.expected: All 40 route file identities from route_audit_manifest.route_file_paths reach terminal status, route_audit_batch_settled wakes the control plane, and final reconciliation can mark the report complete if no duplicates, unknown identities, or unresolved identities exist.
If an expected route file identity never produces a terminal outcome, route_audit_batch_settled does not fire for the full batch and the report cannot receive a complete label.
If two result records represent the same route file identity, reconciliation flags a duplicate and withholds the complete label unless policy later permits otherwise.
If one route audit ends failed, that identity is counted as terminal for await readiness, and reconciliation records it as unresolved under the open failure and partial policies.
If one route audit ends timed_out, that identity is counted as terminal for await readiness, and reconciliation records it as unresolved under the open failure and partial policies.
If a result arrives for a route file identity not present in route_audit_manifest.route_file_paths, it cannot satisfy join readiness and reconciliation flags an unknown identity.
If business policy is later set to reject incomplete outcomes, any missing, failed, cancelled, or timed_out route audit prevents publication of the final report.
If business policy is later set to allow_incomplete, the final report may be published after wake-up with unresolved route file identities explicitly accounted for.

Under an allowed partial policy, report publication may proceed with unresolved expected identities, but the artifact is not labeled complete until complete_label_requires are satisfied.

Because workers currently append to the same report.md sink, the target data plane must serialize writes to at most one concurrent writer or otherwise absorb backpressure without changing control-plane accounting.

edges.reason: await_join needs the launched file identities from the batch manifest to know which audits must settle
shard summaries must wait until all expected file audits reach a terminal status before reducing raw outputs
the final batch reduction consumes shard summaries instead of all raw file audit outputs
final reconciliation verifies the emitted batch summary identities and completeness checks before labeling the batch complete

capacity_constraints.limit: a single final model call must not ingest all raw outputs from 100 file audits

open_points.question: Should duplicate terminal representations for the same file identity be rejected or tolerated with deterministic deduplication?
Should terminal results for file identities not present in the launched manifest be rejected or recorded separately?
May a batch with one or more failed file audits publish a final result, and if so under what labeling rules?
May an incomplete result be published when one or more file identities remain unresolved after cancellation or timeout?

scenarios.expected: All 100 file identities from file_manifest_identity_list reach terminal status, shard summaries are produced, one or more batch summaries are emitted, and final_reconciliation can apply the complete label if all completeness checks pass.

If an expected file identity has no terminal representation, audit_batch_settled does not fire until that identity later becomes cancelled or timed_out; without a terminal record the batch cannot be labeled complete.

If the same expected file identity is represented more than once in terminal accounting, reconciliation records a duplicate condition and withholds the complete label pending the still-open duplicate policy.

If one or more file audits end in failed, audit_batch_settled still fires once all expected identities are terminal, and reconciliation marks those identities unresolved under the still-open failure policy.
If one or more file audits end in timed_out, audit_batch_settled still fires once all expected identities are terminal, and reconciliation marks those identities unresolved for completeness.
If a terminal result arrives for a file identity outside file_manifest_identity_list, it cannot satisfy join readiness and reconciliation records an unknown-identity condition.
If stakeholders choose to refuse partial publication, any unresolved identity causes final_reconciliation to withhold a publishable final result and emit only an incomplete reconciliation outcome.
If stakeholders choose to allow partial publication, the workflow may emit a clearly labeled partial batch summary while still recording unresolved identities in reconciliation.

Because a single final model call cannot safely receive all raw file audit outputs, the data plane reduces raw outputs into shard summaries first, and the control plane reconciles only the resulting summary identities.

scenarios.expected: All 100 launched file identities reach terminal status, shard summaries are produced, one final audit rollup identity is emitted, and reconciliation can mark the batch complete.

If one or more launched file identities never become represented in terminal accounting, await does not settle for those identities and reconciliation reports unresolved identities so the complete label is not satisfied.
If a launched file identity is represented more than once in terminal accounting or reduction input, reconciliation flags duplicates and the publish decision remains open pending duplicate_policy.

If one or more file audits finish with failed status, await may still wake on audit_batch_settled, unresolved accounting includes those identities as needed, and final publication remains open pending failure_policy.

If one or more file audits finish with timed_out status, await may still wake on audit_batch_settled, unresolved accounting includes those identities as needed, and final publication remains open pending failure_policy.

If a terminal record or reduction input references a file identity not present in the launch snapshot, that identity cannot satisfy join eligibility and reconciliation flags an unknown identity pending unknown_policy.

If some file identities are unresolved or intentionally omitted from reductions, reconciliation records the incomplete state and publication is refused under one possible resolution of partial_policy.

If some file identities are unresolved or intentionally omitted from reductions, reconciliation records the incomplete state and publication may proceed with an incomplete label under another possible resolution of partial_policy.

open_points.question: What business action should be taken when the same route identity is represented more than once, such as route-008 appearing twice?
What business action should be taken if the collector receives a terminal record for a route identity outside route-001 through route-060?
May the workflow publish a result when one or more expected routes end in failed, cancelled, or timed_out terminal states?
May an incomplete result be published when reconciliation finds missing or otherwise unresolved route identities?

scenarios.expected: Given route-001 through route-060 each appear exactly once with terminal statuses from the eligible set, await wakes on audit_batch_settled and final reconciliation can apply the complete label because expected_vs_represented, duplicate, unknown, terminal_status, and unresolved checks all pass.

With route-001 through route-060 expected, if route-007 is absent from collector_terminal_records, await does not treat route-007 as settled, final reconciliation withholds the complete label, and collector_unresolved_route_identities includes route-007.

With route-001 through route-060 expected, if route-008 appears twice in collector_terminal_records, final reconciliation records a duplicate anomaly for route-008 and withholds the complete label even if the total received record count is 60.

If any expected route such as route-021 reaches terminal status failed, await may count that identity as settled for readiness, terminal_status_accounted remains true, and any publish decision beyond reconciliation stays governed by the open failure_policy.

If any expected route such as route-034 reaches terminal status timed_out, await may count that identity as settled for readiness, terminal_status_accounted remains true, and any publish decision beyond reconciliation stays governed by the open failure_policy.

If the collector receives a terminal record for an unexpected identity such as route-061, that identity does not satisfy join readiness for any expected route, final reconciliation records an unknown anomaly, and the complete label is withheld.

For the observed case where route-007 is absent and route-008 is duplicated, if partial_policy is later set to reject, the control plane must leave the batch unreconciled as complete and refuse publication of a partial result.

For the observed case where route-007 is absent and route-008 is duplicated, if partial_policy is later set to allow_incomplete, the control plane may publish an explicitly incomplete result while still withholding the complete label and preserving the unresolved and duplicate findings.

open_points.question: When a represented route identity appears more than once, should reconciliation reject the batch or allow an incomplete result to remain visible?

May an incomplete result be published when expected route identities are missing or unresolved?

scenarios.expected: When route-001 through route-060 are each represented exactly once with terminal statuses accounted, the join wakes on audit_batch_settled and reconciliation can mark the batch complete.

Given expected identities route-001 through route-060, if the collector receives 60 records but route-007 is absent, reconciliation reports expected_vs_represented mismatch and unresolved identity route-007, so the batch cannot receive a complete label.

If the collector includes a terminal record for a route identity outside route-001 through route-060, that identity is excluded from join eligibility and reconciliation reports an unknown identity with final disposition policy-open.

edges.reason: quorum readiness is evaluated against the launched child identity snapshot
reconciliation begins after the control plane is woken by the quorum-settled event
reconciliation must compare expected launched identities with represented terminal results

open_points.question: Do failed or timed-out known child identities block reporting, permit incomplete reporting, or allow publication with failure annotations?
May a partial batch report be published after quorum wake when some expected identities remain unresolved or failed?
Should unknown returned identities be rejected from reporting, retained as diagnostics only, or incorporated into an incomplete report?
If the same expected child identity is reported more than once, should reconciliation reject the batch or choose a winner?

scenarios.expected: For a 10-child launch, six succeeded results for six expected identities satisfy quorum k=6 and wake the control plane via audit_batch_settled; reconciliation then reports quorum reached but does not label the batch complete unless all 10 expected identities are cleanly accounted for with no duplicates, no unknown identities, and no unresolved identities.

If any expected child identity has no terminal accounting record, that identity remains unresolved; quorum wake may still occur if six expected identities succeeded, but complete labeling is withheld and reporting behavior remains governed by open partial_policy and failure_policy.

If a terminal result repeats an already represented expected child identity, reconciliation records a duplicate condition; the duplicate does not create extra quorum credit, complete labeling is withheld, and publish-vs-reject remains open under duplicate_policy.

Two failed expected child identities are counted in terminal status accounting and do not satisfy the success quorum; with six succeeded expected identities present, quorum wake still occurs, but whether failed identities permit any final report remains open under failure_policy.

A timed-out expected child identity is counted as terminal for accounting but does not satisfy the success quorum; with six succeeded expected identities present, quorum wake still occurs, and the timed-out identity remains part of unresolved/exception reporting for complete-label checks.

A returned result whose identity is not in launch_manifest_child_identities never satisfies quorum and is flagged as unknown during reconciliation; complete labeling is withheld, and handling of the unknown result remains open under unknown_policy.

If the business owner later chooses to refuse partial reports, the described run wakes on six expected successes but no report is published because failed, timed-out, and unknown/unresolved conditions prevent a complete label.

If the business owner later allows incomplete reporting, the described run wakes on six expected successes and may publish a partial report that explicitly marks two failed expected identities, one timed-out expected identity, and one unknown returned identity while still withholding a complete label.

The terminal outcome carrying an identity outside launch_manifest_child_identities does not satisfy quorum, is flagged during reconciliation as unknown, and causes the no_unknown_identities completeness check to fail.

For the described state of 6 succeeded, 2 failed, 1 timed_out, 1 unknown identity, and 1 missing expected identity, if partial reporting is refused then the control plane may wake on quorum but must not publish a batch report and instead emits only discrepancy and incompleteness findings.

For the described state of 6 succeeded, 2 failed, 1 timed_out, 1 unknown identity, and 1 missing expected identity, if partial reporting is allowed then the control plane may publish an explicitly incomplete batch report after quorum wake, while still recording failed, timed-out, missing, and unknown identity discrepancies and withholding the complete label.

open_points.question: Should duplicate terminal records for the same launched child identity be rejected outright or tolerated with reconciliation annotations?
Should terminal records for identities outside the launch manifest block reporting or be surfaced as non-blocking exceptions?
Do failed or timed-out expected children prevent report publication, or may reporting proceed with exceptions?
May an incomplete result be published when quorum wakes the control plane before all expected identities reconcile cleanly?

scenarios.expected: After six known launched child identities report succeeded, quorum k=6 is satisfied and audit_batch_quorum_or_settled wakes the control plane even though full batch reconciliation is not yet complete.
In the concrete run, only 9 of 10 expected child identities are represented by known terminal records, so final reconciliation marks one expected identity unresolved and withholds the complete label.

If any expected child identity emits more than one terminal record, quorum readiness still evaluates by eligible identity membership, but complete labeling fails the duplicate check and handling remains duplicate_policy=open.
The two failed expected children count as terminal for accounting, but they remain unresolved for completeness; whether their presence blocks reporting remains failure_policy=open.
The timed-out expected child counts as terminal for accounting, but it remains unresolved for completeness; whether its presence blocks reporting remains failure_policy=open.
The terminal record for the identity outside launch_manifest_child_identities never satisfies quorum, is flagged by the unknown check, and contributes to an incomplete reconciliation result.

For the concrete run with 6 succeeded, 2 failed, 1 timed_out, 1 missing expected identity, and 1 unknown identity, if partial publication is refused, the control plane may wake on quorum but publishes no report and records unresolved and unknown findings.

For the concrete run with 6 succeeded, 2 failed, 1 timed_out, 1 missing expected identity, and 1 unknown identity, if partial publication is allowed, the control plane may wake on quorum and publish an explicitly incomplete report annotated with unresolved and unknown findings.
